name: Hoz de Pelegrina and Gollorio Waterfall Loop Trail
name_original: Hoz de Pelegrina y cascada de Gollorio — circular trailrunning por el Parque Natural Barranco del Río Dulce
description: This difficult 4.4-mile (7.1 km) trailrunning route in Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park features the seasonal Gollorio Waterfall.
region_intro: Jogging in Barranco Del Río Dulce offers routes through a landscape shaped by the Dulce River, featuring deep canyons, gorges, and rocky cliffs. The region is characterized by a contrast between these dramatic rock formations and lush riverine forests of holm oaks, poplars, and ash trees. Alcarrian limestone plateaus and smaller tributary valleys also contribute to the varied terrain, providing diverse running experiences. Best jogging routes in Barranco Del Río Dulce The most popular jogging route is Hoz de Pelegrina and Gollorio Waterfall Loop Trail, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete, showcasing the rugged gorge and a seasonal waterfall. Another top favourite among local runners is Gollorio Waterfall – Pelegrina Castle loop from Castillo de Pelegrina, a difficult 4.6 miles (7.5 km) path that includes views of the historic Pelegrina Castle. Local runners also love the Castle of Sigüenza – Sigüenza Castle loop from Sigüenza, a 2.8 miles (4.6 km) trail leading through the historic surroundings of Sigüenza, often completed in about 30 minutes. Jogging in Barranco Del Río Dulce is defined by deep canyons, rocky cliffs, and lush riverine forests. The network of running routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails. The routes in Barranco Del Río Dulce are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 64 runners have used komoot to explore Barranco Del Río Dulce's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: There are over 14 running routes in Barranco Del Río Dulce, offering a variety of experiences through its unique landscapes. These include paths ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or families in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: Yes, Barranco Del Río Dulce offers 3 easy running routes, perfect for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed pace. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40001388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Sigüenza – Sigüenza Castle loop from Sigüenza&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.6 km long and takes around 30 minutes. The park also features inclusive paths like the "Route of the Blinds," designed for broader accessibility.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while jogging in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: While jogging in Barranco Del Río Dulce, you'll encounter dramatic deep canyons and gorges carved by the Dulce River, contrasting with lush riverine forests of holm oaks, poplars, and ash trees. You might also spot unique geological formations like 'enchanted cities' and seasonal waterfalls, especially along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031396569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de Pelegrina and Gollorio Waterfall Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Barranco Del Río Dulce are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031396569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de Pelegrina and Gollorio Waterfall Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollorio Waterfall – Pelegrina Castle loop from Castillo de Pelegrina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks or viewpoints can I discover along the running trails?
A: You can discover several historical landmarks and stunning viewpoints. The ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3399704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pelegrina Castle&lt;/a&gt; are visible on some routes, offering a glimpse into medieval history. For panoramic views, the Mirador de Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente provides outstanding vistas of the park and its soaring birds of prey. The historic town of Sigüenza, with its own castle, also serves as a starting point for several runs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigüenza Castle – Hermitage of the Humilladero loop from Sigüenza&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: While the guide doesn't specify dog-friendly routes, Barranco Del Río Dulce is a natural park, and generally, dogs are permitted on trails if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local regulations or signage at trailheads for any specific restrictions within the park to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see while running in the park?
A: Yes, the park is known for its beautiful seasonal waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3399726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollorio Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable feature, especially after rainfall, and can be enjoyed on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031396569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de Pelegrina and Gollorio Waterfall Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: The running routes in Barranco Del Río Dulce are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the continuous picturesque views, the dramatic canyons, and the serene atmosphere created by the Dulce River. The variety of terrain and the opportunity to spot diverse wildlife, including birds of prey, are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there routes that connect charming villages in the area?
A: Yes, some routes offer the chance to experience the local culture by connecting charming villages within and adjacent to the park. For instance, paths can link villages such as Pelegrina, Aragosa, La Cabrera, and Sigüenza, providing varied terrain and opportunities to explore these picturesque settlements.

Q: What is the typical duration for a running route in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: The duration of running routes in Barranco Del Río Dulce varies depending on the trail's length and difficulty. For example, an easy route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40001388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Sigüenza – Sigüenza Castle loop from Sigüenza&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 30 minutes, while more challenging trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031396569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de Pelegrina and Gollorio Waterfall Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 1 hour.

Q: Is there parking available near the running trailheads?
A: While specific parking locations for each trailhead are not detailed, the charming villages within and around the park, such as Pelegrina and Sigüenza, generally offer parking facilities for visitors. It's recommended to check local information for the specific starting point of your chosen route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: Barranco Del Río Dulce is suitable for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running, with lush greenery and comfortable conditions. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are advisable, while winter offers a different, often quieter, beauty, though some paths might be affected by weather.
tips: Former camp of the team of the naturalist, creator and host of the "Man and the Earth" program.

Waterfall located in the bed of the Gollorio ravine, a tributary of the Dulce River, within the natural park of the Río Dulce Ravine. It is about fifty meters high and only appears from autumn to spring when the rains fill the sources that supply the bed of the ravine.
Although the ruins are few, it is worth contemplating the beauty of the Río Dulce valley at our feet, and understanding the name of the town, from the Latin pellus grinae, beautiful view.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trailrunning route?
faqs.answer: The Pelegrina Gorge and Gollorio Waterfall circular trailrunning route is approximately 7.1 kilometers (4.4 miles) long. While the Komoot moving time is around 1 hour, a more realistic estimate for completing the route, considering its 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, is about 2 hours and 17 minutes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Pelegrina Gorge and Gollorio Waterfall trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and requires good fitness and sure-footedness. While there are mostly accessible paths, you should be prepared for uneven surfaces, loose stones, and slopes, especially near the ravine. A section near the Gollorio Waterfall is even classified as a red difficulty singletrack, indicating narrower, more challenging paths. In times of heavy rainfall, some river crossings might become challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Pelegrina Gorge and Gollorio Waterfall trailrunning route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations aren't detailed, the route starts and ends in Pelegrina. It's advisable to look for parking options within the village of Pelegrina, which serves as the gateway to the Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners in trailrunning?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and the need for good fitness and sure-footedness, this trail is generally not recommended for beginner trailrunners. The varied terrain, including uneven surfaces, slopes, and a red difficulty singletrack section, makes it more suitable for those with some trailrunning experience.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to run this trail and see the Gollorio Waterfall?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit and experience the Gollorio Waterfall in its full glory is from autumn to spring, when rains are abundant. During these seasons, the waterfall, which is 50 meters high, is most active. The natural beauty of the gorge is also vibrant during these cooler months.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Pelegrina Gorge and Gollorio Waterfall trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on trails within the Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park. However, it is always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety and enjoyment of all trail users.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As a natural park, the Barranco del Río Dulce typically does not require specific permits or charge entrance fees for general access to its trails. However, always check local regulations before your visit, especially for any specific activities or protected areas within the park.

faqs.question: What notable sights or landmarks will I encounter along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll find several impressive sights. You'll pass by the Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente Shelter and can enjoy panoramic views from the Mirador Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente. The stunning Gollorio Waterfall is a highlight, as are the impressive rock formations like the 'Mallos de Pelegrina'. You'll also get views of the Pelegrina Castle and the Barranco del Río Dulce canyon itself.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of this trailrunning route coincide with established long-distance paths. You will find sections that are part of the GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT, Camino del Cid (Senderista), and Camino de Santiago de la Lana.Senderista(Var.Sigüenza).

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in the Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park is a haven for wildlife. Keep an eye out for various bird species, including eagles and vultures, which are known to nest in the rocky formations of the gorge. The park's diverse ecosystem supports a range of flora and fauna.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for this trail?
faqs.answer: An insider tip for this trail is to be mindful of river crossings, especially after heavy rainfall, as they can become challenging and might even require turning back. Also, while the Gollorio Waterfall is a major draw, remember it's seasonal, so plan your visit between autumn and spring for the best chance to see it flowing strongly.

waypoints.name: Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente Shelter
